summary: Preinstall hook exfiltrates host metadata to a Burp Collaborator callback
details: On `npm install`, the package's preinstall hook executes index.js, which collects host identifiers (os.hostname(), os.userInfo().username, os.platform(), process.cwd(), Node version, timestamp) and POSTs them as JSON to a hardcoded Burp Collaborator subdomain (3nrgzlqwix6erldow0s0kttsojuai36s.oastify.com/agentcore-poc). package.json declares `"preinstall": "node index.js"`, so the exfiltration fires automatically and without consent for any installer. Although the author labels this an 'agentcore PoC', the behavior is functionally identical to a supply-chain reconnaissance beacon and harms anyone who installs the package.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
